findings.problem: The county-intervention beat is introduced as immediate external pressure, then dissipates without a causal consequence that changes Lena's options. This reads as a suspense feint rather than a structural turn.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the obstruction thread at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:81, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:87, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:151 so the county attempt to enter creates a concrete cost (time loss, forced chain step, written demand, witness conflict, or relocation decision) that directly shapes the later release timeline.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised text anchored around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:81, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:87, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:151 shows one explicit downstream effect from the door challenge that alters procedure or decision timing; fail if the obstruction remains a knock-and-retreat with no later consequence.

findings.problem: Sanitization drift: the discovery sequence leans on bureaucratic categories (petitions, accelerations, transfers, witness lines) while the human cost of dispossession is mostly abstracted. The chapter names harm but often reports it in procedural shorthand at the exact point the narrative should render lived consequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:37, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:45, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:47, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:119, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:123, replace part of the inventory summary with concrete victim-side specifics drawn from the same documents (named household traces, material losses, or coercive conditions) and keep Lena's immediate embodied reaction in-scene rather than only legal categorization.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised material anchored to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:37, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:45, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:47, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:119, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:123 lets a reader identify at least one dispossessed family's concrete circumstance (not just filing type) and shows one direct bodily/social consequence; fail if the passage still reads primarily as docket taxonomy.

findings.problem: In the redaction argument, both speakers deliver polished thesis statements in similar syntax. Under this pressure, the exchange sounds more like position papers than lived, interruptive speech, flattening idiolect separation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the exchange at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:133, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:135, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:137 with sharper spoken texture: keep Mara's fast, evidence-driven pivots and Lena's compact decision rhythm, but add interruption/overlap, contractions, and one line-level lexical contrast per speaker so their voices are distinguishable without tags.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, in the revised exchange anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:133, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:135, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:137, each speaker's turns can be identified by register alone (without dialogue tags), at least one interruption beat appears, and no turn reads as a multi-clause abstract mini-speech; fail if both still sound rhetorically interchangeable.

findings.problem: A recurring stress formula (somatic pain/tightness marker followed by emotional label) appears repeatedly and starts to feel automated, reducing rhythmic variety and emotional precision.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise stress beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:67, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:123, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:209, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:231, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:243 by varying representation mode (action consequence, decision latency, sensory displacement, or memory intrusion) instead of repeating body-part compression cues.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:67, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:123, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:209, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:231, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:243 no longer rely on the same body-distress template and at least two of those beats are externalized through behavior or scene interaction; fail if they remain parallel somatic-emotion constructions.

summary: Chapter 15 lands major causal beats and stays continuity-safe, but it does not clear pass-level standards yet: structural threat payoff is under-realized, the document-discovery passage sanitizes harm into docket abstraction, a key argument scene flattens speaker distinctness, and a repeated somatic stress template dulls prose texture.
